given: A stock record already exists for SKU=X and location=Y with quantity Q1
when: User submits the form to record stock for the same SKU X at the same location Y with a different quantity Q2
then: The existing record is updated with the new quantity Q2 and no error message is shown
architectural_notes: Exercises the R3 uniqueness rule end to end: a second submit for the same (sku, location) upserts rather than duplicating or erroring, asserted via DB state and the absence of a UI error, so E2E. Owner module: StockService owns the create-or-update decision; the unique (sku, location) constraint and idempotent upsert live in StockRepository (the only ORM layer). Defended by the R3 write-time-collision NFR fitness test against the real branch.
